title: WebApp Visual QA and Regression Track
summary: Implement a practical visual QA workflow centered on Playwright native snapshots, deterministic rendering, page/state manifests, and controlled baseline governance. Start with a Chromium-only Phase 1 MVP, then add layout guardrails, CI gating, and advanced coverage.
keyOutcomes: Reliable visual regression checks with low false-positive noise
Coverage for key routes and UI states across critical viewports
Detection of sticky overlap, overflow, and off-page issues
PR-ready artifact workflow for fast visual triage
Controlled baseline update process with explicit approvals

risks.risk: Visual noise from dynamic content causes unstable snapshots
risks.mitigation: Deterministic profile, masking policy, per-page thresholds
Approval workflow, rationale requirement, no automatic updates

risks.risk: CI runtime increases with viewport matrix
risks.mitigation: P0 page set for PR gates, parallelization, optional full runs

dependencies.name: Playwright test runner
dependencies.notes: Native snapshot assertions are core approach

dependencies.name: GitHub Actions
dependencies.notes: Needed for PR gating and artifact publication

blocking: Need final selector contract for strict mobile overlay enforcement in CI
deliverables: Updated webapp-testing skill docs with deterministic visual QA workflow
PR template with baseline rationale at .github/pull_request_template.md
Root npm scripts for visual runs in package.json
Playwright snapshot-based visual checks and baseline policy
Layout guardrails for sticky overlap and overflow/off-page issues
GitHub Actions workflow with visual artifacts and governance
validationCriteria: Phase 1 snapshots run reliably across defined viewports and states
Visual failures produce actionable diffs and HTML report artifacts
Baseline updates require explicit workflow and approval
Layout guardrails catch known synthetic overlap/overflow failures

phases.name: Phase 1: Visual Baseline MVP
phases.focus: Deterministic profile and native snapshot workflow operational with stable baseline and CI scaffold

phases.name: Phase 2: Layout Guardrails
phases.focus: Sticky overlap, off-page, and spacing guardrails integrated with synthetic validation

phases.name: Phase 3: CI/CD and PR Workflow
phases.focus: P0 sharded PR execution, manual run modes, artifact publishing, and baseline rationale/approval-label enforcement

phases.name: Phase 4: Advanced Expansion
phases.focus: Cross-browser baselines validated, five non-blocking CI runs recorded, and default mode promoted to blocking
